response.description_long:
HVAC is typically the largest energy load in an office or retail building, yet most systems run on static schedules programmed years ago. Buildings heat empty floors, pre-cool on mild days, and fight themselves between zones. Gridmaple addresses this waste by optimizing HVAC operations without requiring new hardware.

The software connects to existing building management systems (BMS) over BACnet — no sensors to install, no retrofit. Machine-learning models forecast occupancy and weather for each zone and compute an optimized schedule every 15 minutes. Changes flow back to the BMS within guardrails set by the facilities team, and every adjustment is logged and reversible. Utility-rate awareness shifts load away from peak pricing and demand charges.

Customers measure energy savings of 15 to 30 percent on HVAC, verified against utility meters. Portfolios also use Gridmaple reporting for emissions disclosures and utility rebate programs. The company serves owners and operators of office, retail, and healthcare portfolios — from a single 80,000-square-foot building to portfolios of 200 sites.

Gridmaple is headquartered in New York City, founded in 2021 by controls engineers and a former utility analyst. It employs 52 people. Pricing is per building per year, sized by floor area.
